name: Assorted Vegetables in a Clear Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Crab meat or shelled shrimp (opt)
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Peel turnips and carrots. Use a melon scoop to cut turnips into large balls. Slice carrots 1/2 inch thick; then with a paring knife, cut 4 evenly spaced notches into the rim of each slice (do not cut into the center core). Carrots should look like little flowers. Parboil turnip and carrot pieces in stock until barely tender. Remove from stock and plunge pieces into cold water; drain. Cut onions, asparagus, and baby corn into 1 1/2 inch pieces.
Stir-frying:
1. Add peanut oil to a hot wok.
2. When the oil begins to smoke, briskly fry crab meat or shrimp for 1 minute.
3. Add asparagus, baby corn, mushrooms, and water chestnuts; stir-fry until they are hot.
4. Add ginger mixture, then onions. Stir another 30 seconds.
5. Add 1/2 cup chicken stock, salt, and sugar; bring to boil.
6. Add turnip and carrot pieces. Cover and reduce heat; simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Uncover, push ingredients out of the liquid, and dribble in cornstarch paste to thicken slightly.
8. Stir the liquid to prevent lumping while it thickens into a thick soup.
9. Recombine all ingredients, then mix in chicken fat.
10. Remove to serving platter.
